name: openstaad-mcp
description: MCP server for Bentley STAAD.Pro via the OpenSTAAD API
long_description: Bridges AI agents to a running Bentley STAAD.Pro instance through the OpenSTAAD API. Provides tools to query API documentation, execute validated JavaScript code against the live model inside a WebAssembly sandbox, and check connection health. Ships as a self-contained Windows executable — no Python installation required.

tools.name: discover_api
tools.description: Discover available API guidance and skills. Call this before using other openstaad-mcp tools to understand the API surface and see what skills are available. Then use ``read_skills`` with one or more specific skill names to load full guidance.

tools.name: read_skills
tools.description: Read one or more skills by name. Use ``discover_api`` first to list available skills. Each skill provides domain-specific guidance (e.g. analysis, geometry, loads). Pass skill names like ``['staad-analysis']`` or sub-paths like ``['staad-design/assets/STEEL_CODES']`` to read reference files within a skill.

tools.name: list_instances
tools.description: List currently active STAAD instances. Returns instance IDs, model paths, and STAAD versions. Use instance IDs to target specific models in other tools.

tools.name: execute_code
tools.description: Execute JavaScript code against the OpenSTAAD API. The sandbox provides a pre-connected 'staad' object (the OpenSTAAD root) plus `console`. Filesystem, network, and module imports are physically unreachable — code runs inside a WebAssembly isolate. The last expression value or an explicit `return <value>` is returned as the result; use console.log / console.error for progress output.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Check the connection to STAAD.Pro. Returns connection state, STAAD version, model path, and analysis status.

tools.name: read_analysis_output
tools.description: Read the analysis output file (.ANL) or solver log (.LOG) for the currently open model. Path is derived automatically from the open model. Use this to access concrete, timber, and aluminum design results which are not available through COM.
